# Packages and Repositories

Phase: Apply

Stage packages, configure repositories, refresh metadata, and install packages.

## Summary

- phase: `apply`
- group: `packages-repositories`
- kinds: [ConfigureRepository](#configurerepository), [RefreshRepository](#refreshrepository), [InstallPackage](#installpackage)

## When To Use This Group

Use this group for package workflows that start with prepare-time package staging and finish with apply-time repository setup and installation.

## Typical Flows

### Install from offline repositories during apply

- kinds: [ConfigureRepository](#configurerepository), [RefreshRepository](#refreshrepository), [InstallPackage](#installpackage)

## Shared Step Fields

Shared step envelope fields such as `id`, `apiVersion`, `kind`, `when`, `parallelGroup`, `retry`, `timeout`, `register`, and `metadata` are documented in [Step Envelope Contract](../../workflow-model.md#step-envelope-contract).

## Supported Kinds

- [ConfigureRepository](#configurerepository): Write deb or rpm repository definitions.
- [RefreshRepository](#refreshrepository): Refresh package metadata with repo filtering.
- [InstallPackage](#installpackage): Install packages on the local node.

## `ConfigureRepository`

Write deb or rpm repository definitions.

- outputs: `path`

### When To Use

Use this before refreshing caches or installing packages from a local mirror.

### Example

```yaml
kind: ConfigureRepository
spec:
  format: deb
  path: /etc/apt/sources.list.d/offline.list
  repositories:
    - baseurl: http://repo.local/debian
      trusted: true
```

### Spec Fields

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.backupPaths` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Paths to back up before modifying. | `[/etc/apt/sources.list]` |
| `spec.cleanupPaths` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Paths to remove before writing the new definition. | `[/etc/apt/sources.list.d/ubuntu.list]` |
| `spec.disableExisting` | `boolean` | no | `` | `` | Disable all existing repository definitions before writing the new one. | `true` |
| `spec.format` | `string` | no | `` | `auto, deb, rpm` | Repository file format to write. | `deb` |
| `spec.mode` | `string` | no | `` | `` | File permissions applied to the generated repository file. | `0644` |
| `spec.path` | `string` | no | `` | `` | Explicit output path for the generated repository file. | `/etc/apt/sources.list.d/offline.list` |
| `spec.replaceExisting` | `boolean` | no | `` | `` | Replace an existing repository file at the target path. | `true` |
| `spec.repositories` | `array<object>` | yes | `` | `` | Repository entries to write. | `[{baseurl:http://repo.local/debian,trusted:true}]` |

### Notes

- `ConfigureRepository` only writes repository definition files. Use `RefreshRepository` when the package manager needs an explicit metadata refresh.
- Keep repository definitions mirror-specific rather than mutating the host's default online sources.
- Repository entries need a usable identity for the selected format: `format: deb` entries require `baseurl`; `format: rpm` entries require `id`.

## `RefreshRepository`

Refresh package metadata with repo filtering.


### When To Use

Use this after writing repo definitions and before package install steps that depend on fresh metadata.

### Example

```yaml
kind: RefreshRepository
spec:
  manager: apt
  clean: true
  update: true
  restrictToRepos:
    - /etc/apt/sources.list.d/offline.list
```

### Spec Fields

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.clean` | `boolean` | no | `` | `` | Run a cache clean before updating metadata. | `true` |
| `spec.excludeRepos` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Repository selectors to skip during metadata update. | `[updates]` |
| `spec.manager` | `string` | no | `` | `auto, apt, dnf` | Package manager to use. | `apt` |
| `spec.restrictToRepos` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Limit the metadata update to these repository selectors. | `[/etc/apt/sources.list.d/offline.list]` |
| `spec.update` | `boolean` | no | `` | `` | Fetch fresh package metadata from the configured repositories. | `true` |

### Validation Rules

- At least one of `spec.clean` or `spec.update` must be set.
- At least one of the listed branches must match.

### Notes

- Use `RefreshRepository` after writing repo definitions and before package installs that depend on fresh metadata.

## `InstallPackage`

Install packages on the local node.


### When To Use

Use this during apply to install packages from configured local or mirrored repositories.

### Example

```yaml
kind: InstallPackage
spec:
  packages: [kubelet, kubeadm, kubectl]
  source:
    type: local-repo
    path: /opt/deck/repos/kubernetes
```

### Spec Fields

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.excludeRepos` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Repository selectors to exclude from package resolution. | `[updates]` |
| `spec.packages` | `array<string>` | yes | `` | `` | Package names to install. | `[kubelet,kubeadm,kubectl]` |
| `spec.restrictToRepos` | `array<string>` | no | `` | `` | Limit package manager visibility to these repository selectors. | `[offline-kubernetes]` |
| `spec.source` | `object` | no | `` | `` | Local repository source used for the installation. | `{type:local-repo,path:/opt/deck/repos/kubernetes}` |

### Nested Objects

### `spec.source`

| Key | Type | Required | Default | Enum | Description | Example |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `spec.source.path` | `string` | yes | `` | `` | Filesystem path to the pre-prepared local package repository. | `/opt/deck/repos/kubernetes` |
| `spec.source.type` | `string` | yes | `` | `` | Source type. Currently only `local-repo` is supported. | `local-repo` |


### Notes

- Use `InstallPackage` during apply to install packages from configured local or mirrored repositories.
